You won't find an AI chatbot on Nordic's website or TI or STMicro or any of these companies, which is fascinating because their sales and support cycles are 54 to 87 weeks long.
The buying team is 18 to 24 people, and they're almost exclusively looking to do support to make that decision if they're going to buy.
So those 18 to 24 people, on average, if we were selling to the next Google Home Hub, that buying team would hit our website between 7 and 9,000 times just through the opportunities.
And it wasn't for marketing cloud or it wasn't for webinars, it was for support content.
So I say that to highlight the industry is really good at solving advanced problems for the world, but not very good at adopting new tech.

technology, doesn't know how to adopt it because they don't have good internal teams, or in the instance of the main problem that we're solving, which is the second point I was going to make, a lot of manufacturing companies lean really heavily on external partner networks, so at Silicon Labs we had 25,000 customers, a thousand were managed by our direct sales team, the other 24,000 were managed by external distribution partners, we had 40 globally, and it was everybody from Aero Semiconductor, who's in Denver that's 27 billion a year in revenue, down to, it's not quite mom and pop, but it's 200 sales sales people that are working five lines to cover the Southwest.
And the biggest challenges in those relationships are sales acumen and technical acumen.

And we had three mechanisms to solve that.
So email, phone call, and a custom CMS.
So if I'm a technician, and I'm at this dealership, and I'm on site with a customer, and I'm trying to figure out a problem, I'm going to pick up the phone, and I'm going call.
I'll call an unlucky product manager in Silicon Labs and hope he picks up.

If that doesn't work, I'm finally going to go to the custom CMS, I'm going to go through 10,000 documents, and I'm going to try to find a manual that's 180 pages long and find problems on that.
So Silicon Labs had this problem.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory call between GrowthX and Circuit to explore potential collaboration on AI-driven content creation and growth strategies.](https://fathom.video/share/KZpZ_HxTo_kN_ixUUHBBwZ9w1c8kg3ba?tab=summary&timestamp=1.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Circuit, a year-old startup, specializes in AI solutions for manufacturing and industrial sectors, focusing on technical support and distribution management.](https://fathom.video/share/KZpZ_HxTo_kN_ixUUHBBwZ9w1c8kg3ba?tab=summary&timestamp=2.0)
  - [GrowthX offers AI-powered content creation services, with a two-phase approach: initial sprint for strategy and artifact creation, followed by growth execution.](https://fathom.video/share/KZpZ_HxTo_kN_ixUUHBBwZ9w1c8kg3ba?tab=summary&timestamp=8.0)
  - [Circuit is interested in expanding their content strategy to target SMB/mid-market segments and establish thought leadership in their space.](https://fathom.video/share/KZpZ_HxTo_kN_ixUUHBBwZ9w1c8kg3ba?tab=summary&timestamp=1533.0)
  - [A follow-up meeting is scheduled for Monday at 11 AM CST to discuss GrowthX's platform and potential collaboration in more detail.](https://fathom.video/share/KZpZ_HxTo_kN_ixUUHBBwZ9w1c8kg3ba?tab=summary&timestamp=1556.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX Background and Offerings

  - [Founded over a year ago, now at 70 employees and $7+ million run rate](https://fathom.video/share/KZpZ_HxTo_kN_ixUUHBBwZ9w1c8kg3ba?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Focuses on AI-driven content creation to help companies build inbound engines](https://fathom.video/share/KZpZ_HxTo_kN_ixUUHBBwZ9w1c8kg3ba?tab=summary&timestamp=1223.0)
  - [Two-phase approach: Initial sprint for strategy/artifact creation, followed by growth execution](https://fathom.video/share/KZpZ_HxTo_kN_ixUUHBBwZ9w1c8kg3ba?tab=summary&timestamp=1260.0)
  - [Typical engagement includes 5-15 articles per week, with some custom options available](https://fathom.video/share/KZpZ_HxTo_kN_ixUUHBBwZ9w1c8kg3ba?tab=summary&timestamp=1483.0)
